title: Ο περί Προστασίας Εμπιστευτικών Πληροφοριών (Επιχειρηματικά Απόρρητα) Νόμος
title_en: Protection of Confidential Business Information (Trade Secrets) Law
description: This Law transposes Directive (EU) 2016/943 on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use, and disclosure into Cypriot law. It establishes a harmonised legal framework for the protection of trade secrets, defines lawful and unlawful acts in relation to trade secrets, and provides civil remedies for holders of trade secrets whose rights have been infringed. The Law balances the legitimate interests of trade secret holders against the public interest in transparency, freedom of expression, and employee mobility.

provisions.title: Article 1. Subject Matter and Scope
provisions.content: This Law establishes the rules for the protection of trade secrets against their unlawful acquisition, use, and disclosure, transposing Directive (EU) 2016/943 of the European Parliament and of the Council on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use and disclosure. The Law applies to the unlawful acquisition, use, and disclosure of trade secrets occurring within the territory of the Republic of Cyprus. This Law shall not be interpreted so as to restrict the freedom of expression and information as guaranteed by the Constitution of the Republic, to restrict the disclosure of wrongdoing in the public interest, or to restrict rights of workers' representatives to be informed and consulted under applicable labour law.

provisions.title: Article 2. Definitions
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Law: 'trade secret' means information which meets all of the following requirements: it is secret in the sense that it is not, as a body or in the precise configuration and assembly of its components, generally known among or readily accessible to persons within the circles that normally deal with the kind of information in question; it has commercial value because it is secret; and it has been subject to reasonable steps under the circumstances, by the person lawfully in control of the information, to keep it secret. 'Trade secret holder' means any natural or legal person lawfully controlling a trade secret. 'Infringer' means any natural or legal person who has unlawfully acquired, used or disclosed a trade secret.

provisions.title: Article 3. Lawful Acquisition, Use, and Disclosure
provisions.content: The acquisition of a trade secret shall be considered lawful when it is obtained through independent discovery or creation; through observation, study, disassembly, or testing of a product or object that has been made available to the public or is lawfully in the possession of the acquirer who is free from any legally valid duty to limit the acquisition of the trade secret; through exercise of the right of workers or workers' representatives to information and consultation; or through any other practice which, under the circumstances, is in conformity with honest commercial practices. Disclosure of a trade secret for the purpose of revealing misconduct, wrongdoing, or illegal activity, provided it was carried out for the purpose of protecting the general public interest, shall be lawful.

provisions.title: Article 4. Unlawful Acquisition, Use, and Disclosure
provisions.content: The acquisition of a trade secret without the consent of the trade secret holder shall be considered unlawful whenever it results from unauthorised access to, or copying of, documents, objects, materials, substances, or electronic files; from any other conduct which, under the circumstances, is considered contrary to honest commercial practices. The use or disclosure of a trade secret shall be considered unlawful when it is carried out without the consent of the trade secret holder by a person who is shown to have acquired the trade secret unlawfully; to be in breach of a confidentiality agreement or any other duty not to disclose the trade secret; or to be in breach of a contractual or any other duty to limit the use of the trade secret.

provisions.title: Article 5. Exceptions and Limitations
provisions.content: Measures, procedures, and remedies provided for in this Law shall not be applied in cases where the alleged acquisition, use, or disclosure of a trade secret was carried out for exercising the right to freedom of expression and information as established in the Constitution and applicable Union law; for revealing misconduct, wrongdoing, or illegal activity, provided that the respondent acted for the purpose of protecting the general public interest; for disclosure by workers to their representatives as part of the legitimate exercise by those representatives of their functions in accordance with Union or national law; or for the purpose of protecting a legitimate interest recognised by Union or national law. The pursuit of the exceptions set out in this article shall be assessed in the light of the circumstances of each case.

provisions.title: Article 6. Civil Remedies
provisions.content: Trade secret holders whose rights under this Law have been infringed shall be entitled to apply to the competent courts for the following civil remedies: the cessation of or prohibition on the use or disclosure of the trade secret; the prohibition on the production, offering, or placing on the market of infringing goods; the adoption of appropriate corrective measures with regard to infringing goods; the destruction of documents, objects, materials, substances, or electronic files that contain or embody the trade secret; or any other measure that the court considers appropriate in the circumstances. Courts shall assess the proportionality of remedies having regard to the specific circumstances of the case, including the value of the trade secret, the measures taken by the holder to protect it, and the significance of the infringement.

provisions.title: Article 7. Interim and Precautionary Measures
provisions.content: On application by the trade secret holder, the competent courts may order interim or precautionary measures against an alleged infringer, including the immediate cessation of use or disclosure of the trade secret on a provisional basis, the prohibition of the production or marketing of infringing goods, the seizure or delivery of infringing goods to prevent their entry into or circulation in the market, or the provision of a security or equivalent assurance by the alleged infringer. Courts shall have the power to require the applicant to provide security or an equivalent assurance sufficient to prevent abuse of the procedure and to ensure that the alleged infringer is compensated for any prejudice suffered in the event that the application is dismissed.

provisions.title: Article 8. Damages
provisions.content: Competent courts may, on the application of the injured party, order an infringer who knew or ought to have known that they were engaging in unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of a trade secret to pay the trade secret holder damages appropriate to the actual prejudice suffered as a result of the unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of the trade secret. In setting the damages, courts shall take into account all appropriate factors, such as the negative economic consequences, including lost profits, suffered by the injured party, any unfair profits made by the infringer, and, in appropriate cases, elements other than economic factors such as the moral prejudice caused to the trade secret holder. The court may, as an alternative, set damages as a lump sum on the basis of elements such as the amount of royalties which would have been due if the infringer had requested authorisation to use the trade secret concerned.

provisions.title: Article 9. Confidentiality in Legal Proceedings
provisions.content: Competent courts may, on a duly reasoned application by a party or on their own initiative in duly justified cases, take specific measures necessary to preserve the confidentiality of any trade secret or alleged trade secret used or referred to in the course of legal proceedings relating to the unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of a trade secret. Such measures may include restricting access to documents or hearings containing or referring to trade secrets to a limited number of persons, and imposing obligations on the persons permitted access to keep the information confidential. Measures restricting public access to hearings or court documents shall be balanced against the principle of open justice and shall be limited to what is strictly necessary to preserve the confidentiality of the trade secret.

provisions.title: Article 10. Statute of Limitations
provisions.content: Claims for civil remedies under this Law shall be subject to a limitation period of six years from the date on which the trade secret holder became aware, or had reason to become aware, of the last act of unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure giving rise to the claim. The limitation period shall be suspended or interrupted in accordance with the general rules of Cypriot law applicable to civil claims. For the purposes of this Law, continuing or repeated acts of unlawful use or disclosure shall each give rise to a fresh cause of action, and the limitation period shall run separately in respect of each such act.

provisions.title: Article 11. Protection of Whistleblowers and Journalists
provisions.content: This Law shall be applied in a manner that does not restrict the protection afforded to whistleblowers and journalists under applicable law. Measures, procedures, and remedies provided for in this Law shall not be applied against persons who revealed or disclosed a trade secret in the public interest, in particular where the disclosure relates to misconduct, fraud, or unlawful activity by the trade secret holder or by persons acting on its behalf, where the respondent acted without intent of obtaining economic advantage, and where such disclosure did not go beyond what was strictly necessary for the purpose of revealing the wrongdoing or unlawful activity. Courts shall take account of the public interest in transparency and accountability when assessing applications for remedies in cases involving alleged whistleblowing or journalistic activity.

provisions.title: Article 12. Employee Mobility
provisions.content: This Law shall not restrict the mobility of employees and shall not be applied so as to prevent employees from using the knowledge, skills, and experience honestly acquired in the normal course of their employment. Post-employment restrictions on the use of information by former employees shall be governed by the provisions of applicable labour law and shall not be extended beyond what is proportionate and necessary to protect the legitimate business interests of the employer. Courts shall take account of the right of employees to use their general knowledge and skills acquired in the course of their employment and of the public interest in the free movement of workers when considering applications for remedies against former employees.

provisions.title: Article 13. Enforcement and Jurisdiction
provisions.content: The competent courts of the Republic of Cyprus shall have jurisdiction over claims under this Law arising from unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of trade secrets occurring within the territory of Cyprus, and in cross-border cases in accordance with applicable rules of private international law and Union law. In proceedings relating to trade secrets, courts shall have access to appropriate technical expertise and may appoint expert witnesses to assist in the assessment of technical or commercial matters relevant to the claim. The Republic of Cyprus shall cooperate with other Member States in the enforcement of rights relating to trade secrets and in the collection of evidence located in other jurisdictions in accordance with applicable mutual assistance arrangements.

provisions.title: Article 14. Relationship with Other Legal Frameworks
provisions.content: The provisions of this Law apply without prejudice to the exercise of any right to freedom of expression and information, including respect for the freedom and pluralism of the media, as guaranteed by the Constitution of the Republic of Cyprus and by Union law. This Law applies without prejudice to the application of rules on data protection, including Regulation (EU) 2016/679, and to the protection of personal data processed in the context of commercial activities. The protection of trade secrets provided under this Law is without prejudice to the protection of intellectual property rights, including patents, supplementary protection certificates, plant variety rights, designs, trademarks, and copyright.

provisions.title: Article 15. Reporting and Review
provisions.content: The Ministry of Energy, Commerce, and Industry shall monitor the application of this Law and shall report to the European Commission on the experience acquired and the effectiveness of measures taken to transpose Directive (EU) 2016/943 in accordance with the reporting obligations established therein. The Ministry shall carry out a review of the operation of this Law at intervals of not less than five years and shall consult with stakeholders, including business associations, employee organisations, and civil society, in conducting such reviews. The Ministry shall notify the European Commission of any significant developments in the application of this Law affecting the protection of trade secrets or the operation of the internal market.

provisions.title: Article 16. Sanctions for Abuse of Proceedings
provisions.content: Competent courts may, upon application of the defendant, impose sanctions on trade secret holders who bring proceedings under this Law abusively or in bad faith, including the award of costs and legal fees to the defendant, the payment of damages to the defendant for any harm suffered as a result of the abusive or bad faith proceedings, and the publication of judicial decisions relating to the abuse or bad faith conduct. The availability of sanctions for abuse of proceedings shall be applied in a manner that does not discourage legitimate recourse to the civil remedies provided under this Law and that takes account of the difficulty in assessing, at the time of bringing proceedings, whether the alleged infringement actually occurred.

definitions.definition: Information that is secret (not generally known or readily accessible), has commercial value because it is secret, and has been subject to reasonable steps to keep it secret by the person lawfully controlling it.
Any natural or legal person lawfully controlling a trade secret.
Any natural or legal person who has unlawfully acquired, used, or disclosed a trade secret.
Goods whose design, characteristics, functioning, production process or marketing significantly benefits from trade secrets unlawfully acquired, used, or disclosed.
